title: [MacOS 14.5]no space left on device
body:
### Issue Description

Got the `no space` issue after `rm` old machine, more: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23045
```console
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ podman build -t quay.io/olmqe/etcd-index:fips -f catalog.Dockerfile 
Error: mkdir /var/tmp/libpod_builder3448793565: no space left on device

jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ podman machine list 
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED      LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default*  applehv     3 weeks ago  Currently running  5           2GiB        100GiB

jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ 
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ podman images
REPOSITORY  TAG         IMAGE ID    CREATED     SIZE
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ podman images -a
REPOSITORY  TAG         IMAGE ID    CREATED     SIZE
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ sudo podman ps -a
Password:
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ sudo podman images
REPOSITORY  TAG         IMAGE ID    CREATED     SIZE
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  following https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23045#issuecomment-2178274543 
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received
```console
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ podman build -t quay.io/olmqe/etcd-index:fips -f catalog.Dockerfile 
Error: mkdir /var/tmp/libpod_builder3448793565: no space left on device
```

### Describe the results you expected

The `podman` works.

comments.body: I have pretty much the same issue

> Error: mkdir /var/tmp/libpod_builder3448793565: no space left on device

The error message is on /var/tmp, how does that look? (free space (df), used space (du) etc)

I have no clue how apple designed things but it's probably a separate partition from the rest of the podman storage.
It's possible you have old podman directories around from previous builds there, you can delete them
Is the space problem on the MAC or within the machine?

This looks like a lot of garbage collected within the machine.  `podman machine ssh `

And see what is leaking all over /var/tmp

Hi, I recreate a machine with 50G, but still encounter this issue, as follows, 
```console
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ podman machine list
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED             LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default*  applehv     About a minute ago  Currently running  5           2GiB        50GiB
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ podman build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64,linux/ppc64le,linux/s390x  -t quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-index:33450 -f catalog.Dockerfile 
Error: write /var/tmp/libpod_builder1195209327/build/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv/podman-machine-default-arm64.raw: no space left on device
```
The machine error log.
```console
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 40.20240701.2.0
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

core@localhost:~$

core@localhost:~$ journalctl -p err
Jul 16 17:33:39 localhost systemd[1]: /etc/systemd/system/var-folders.automount:2: Where= path is not absolute, ignoring: a0bb3a2c8b0b02ba5958b0576f0d6530e104
Jul 16 17:33:39 localhost systemd[1]: /etc/systemd/system/private.automount:2: Where= path is not absolute, ignoring: 71708eb255bc230cd7c91dd26f7667a7b938

Jul 16 17:33:40 localhost systemd-tmpfiles[1599]: "/srv" already exists and is not a directory.
Jul 16 17:33:40 localhost systemd-tmpfiles[1599]: "/root" already exists and is not a directory.

```
And, seems like the space is good.
```console
core@localhost:~$ df -h
Filesystem                            Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/vda4                              50G  3.3G   47G   7% /sysroot
devtmpfs                              4.0M     0  4.0M   0% /dev

/dev/vda3                             350M  134M  194M  41% /boot

core@localhost:~$ df -h /var/tmp/
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/vda4        50G  3.3G   47G   7% /var
```

> It's possible you have old podman directories around from previous builds there, you can delete them

Hi @martinetd , how to delete them? Can you describe the exact folders? Or any docs? Thanks! I uninstall the old podman, and install a new one, but still encounter this issue.
```console
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ podman --version
podman version 5.1.1

jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ df -h /var/tmp
Filesystem      Size    Used   Avail Capacity iused ifree %iused  Mounted on
/dev/disk3s1   460Gi   202Gi   239Gi    46%    3.3M  2.5G    0%   /System/Volumes/Data
```

> And see what is leaking all over /var/tmp

Which unit should I check?
```console
core@localhost:~$ ls -hl /var/tmp/
total 0
drwx------. 3 root root 17 Jul 16 17:33 systemd-private-98bb43535a2540c6b9863a51256726c9-chronyd.service-6MD8xm

drwx------. 3 root root 17 Jul 16 17:33 systemd-private-98bb43535a2540c6b9863a51256726c9-irqbalance.service-bwTQMU
drwx------. 3 root root 17 Jul 16 18:40 systemd-private-98bb43535a2540c6b9863a51256726c9-polkit.service-OhlrAZ
drwx------. 3 root root 17 Jul 16 17:33 systemd-private-98bb43535a2540c6b9863a51256726c9-systemd-logind.service-5BcsrD
drwx------. 3 root root 17 Jul 16 17:33 systemd-private-98bb43535a2540c6b9863a51256726c9-systemd-resolved.service-zssFEO

core@localhost:~$ systemctl status podman 
○ podman.service - Podman API Service
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/podman.service; disabled; preset: disabled)
    Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/system/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
     Active: inactive (dead)
TriggeredBy: ● podman.socket
       Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
```
How big is your context dir? The full context dir must be copied into the VM on build.
Also update to podman 5.1 and create a new machine, if I remember correctly there were some improvements around the remote copy handling for build in recent versions.

> The full context dir must be copied into the VM on build.

Hi @Luap99 , many thanks for your info! I copied it to a new folder, and **it works now!** 

```console
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ tree catalog
catalog
└── etcd
    └── catalog.json

2 directories, 1 file
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ du -h catalog
 28K	catalog/etcd
 28K	catalog

jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ df -h .
Filesystem      Size    Used   Avail Capacity iused ifree %iused  Mounted on
/dev/disk3s1   460Gi   204Gi   237Gi    47%    3.3M  2.5G    0%   /System/Volumes/Data


jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ cd test
jiazha-mac:test jiazha$ cp -r ../catalog .
jiazha-mac:test jiazha$ cp ../catalog.Dockerfile .

jiazha-mac:test jiazha$ du -h .
 16K	./auth
 28K	./catalog/etcd
 28K	./catalog
 64K	./openshift
 56K	./manifests
1.8M	.
```
But, why? It's really confusing, as you can see, in the Dockerfile, I just `ADD` a `catalog` folder, not all context dir.
```console
jiazha-mac:~ jiazha$ cat catalog.Dockerfile 
# The base image is expected to contain
# /bin/opm (with a serve subcommand) and /bin/grpc_health_probe
FROM registry.redhat.io/openshift4/ose-operator-registry-rhel9:v4.16

# Configure the entrypoint and command
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/opm"]
CMD ["serve", "/configs", "--cache-dir=/tmp/cache"]

# Copy declarative config root into image at /configs and pre-populate serve cache
ADD catalog /configs
RUN ["/bin/opm", "serve", "/configs", "--cache-dir=/tmp/cache", "--cache-only"]

# Set DC-specific label for the location of the DC root directory
# in the image
LABEL operators.operatorframework.io.index.configs.v1=/configs
```
Hi @Luap99 , could you help look at this following issue https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23319 when you get a chance? Thanks!
if it works for you now, can we close the issue?
Sure, but I have concerns about this `The full context dir must be copied into the VM on build.`, it's really not a good experience.

@jianzhangbjz: Closing this issue.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23287#issuecomment-2236015839):

>/close


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes-sigs/prow](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/prow/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

The error we give when this happens would be nice to tell the user that. Basically during the build when expanding the contextdir we should give an error telling the user that the contextdir is too big.

BTW Docker has the same issue.  When used in client server mode, there is no way to know what files are going to be used within the contextdir, which is why we must copy it all in. You can use .containerignore and .dockerignore to limit the copying.  But often it is better to move the Containerfile to its own directory.

Podman on linux does not have this issue.
